name: Tying a Bowline Knot
description: Tying a secure, non-slip loop at the end of a rope. Ideal for rescue, maritime, and utility situations.

steps.name: Form the Eye
steps.description: Form a small, neat loop (the 'eye' or 'rabbit hole') in the standing part of the rope. Ensure the working end rests on top.
steps.voice_guideline: Welcome to Bowline Knot coaching. Step 1: Form the Eye. Twist a small, neat loop in the standing line, making sure the active end lies on top.
steps.illustrative_image_prompt: Vector drawing of a hand forming a perfect clockwise twist loop in a blue marine rope on a clean background.

checklist.title: Clean Loop Created
checklist.description: A clean loop is formed in the main line of the rope

checklist.title: Working End on Top
checklist.description: The active working end of the rope lies across the top of the standing line

failure_conditions.description: The loop is twisted backwards, causing the rabbit hole to collapse
failure_conditions.trigger_description: Loop formed with working end under the standing part
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Flip your loop so the working end rests on top of the standing rope, not behind it.

steps.name: Rabbit Comes Out of the Hole
steps.description: Bring the working end (the 'rabbit') up through the loop (the 'hole') from the bottom side.
steps.voice_guideline: Excellent. Now, Step 2: Pass the working end up through the loop from underneath, just like a rabbit emerging from its hole.
steps.illustrative_image_prompt: Vector drawing showing the active rope end passing up through the center of the previously formed loop from behind.

checklist.title: Rabbit Through Hole
checklist.description: The working end has emerged fully from the bottom side of the loop to the top side

failure_conditions.description: Working end entered from the top side instead of the bottom side
failure_conditions.trigger_description: Rabbit went down into the hole instead of coming up out of it
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Take the working end back out. Thread it up through the loop from underneath.

steps.name: Around the Tree
steps.description: Pass the working end behind the standing part of the rope (the 'tree') from right to left.
steps.voice_guideline: Step 3: Take the rabbit around the tree. Wrap the active rope end behind the standing main line from right to left.
steps.illustrative_image_prompt: Vector drawing of the rope end crossing behind the main vertical rope line and wrapping around the back.

checklist.title: Around Standing Line
checklist.description: The working end of the rope has wrapped completely behind the main standing line

failure_conditions.description: Working end passed in front of the tree instead of behind it
failure_conditions.trigger_description: Working end crossed over the front of the standing line
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: You crossed in front of the main line. Pull it back and wrap it around the back of the rope.

steps.name: Back Down the Hole & Tighten
steps.description: Pass the working end back down into the loop and pull the active ends to tighten the knot.
steps.voice_guideline: Final Step: Feed the rabbit back down into the hole and pull firmly on both parts to lock the knot in place.
steps.illustrative_image_prompt: Vector drawing of the rope end entering back into the original loop, with arrows showing hands pulling the knot tight.

checklist.title: Back Into Loop
checklist.description: Working end is threaded back down through the original loop

checklist.title: Knot Secured
checklist.description: The loop is tight and the structural shape of the bowline is locked

failure_conditions.description: The working end missed the loop during return
failure_conditions.trigger_description: Working end is pulled tight outside the loop
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Thread the working end back down into the small loop before tightening.
